title: Canonical Paths + Remote-Safe Bootstrap
objective: Make `start-slice.sh` work from canonicalized paths and in repos that do not yet have `origin/<base_branch>`.
description: The current bootstrap flow breaks when the repo root and the input slice path resolve through different canonical forms, such as `/tmp` vs `/private/tmp` on macOS. It also assumes `origin/<base_branch>` exists, which is false in a freshly initialized repo. This slice makes the bootstrap path deterministic and replaces raw git failures with actionable behavior.
must: Normalize `repo_root`, the input slice path, and derived relative paths before validating that the slice lives under `specs/` or `specs-fix/`
Support bootstrapping from a local `develop` or `main` base branch when `origin` is missing
Fail with a clear actionable message when neither the declared base branch nor a compatible fallback exists
Keep the existing branch strategy rules (`feature|bugfix -> develop`, `hotfix -> main`) intact
not_included: Template placeholder cleanup outside the bootstrap path
acceptance: Running `start-slice.sh` with a slice path reached through a symlinked directory does not misclassify the slice as outside `specs/`
Running `start-slice.sh` in a fresh repo with only a local `develop` branch creates the worktree successfully
When the declared base branch is missing, the script exits with an actionable error message instead of a raw git failure
The resulting worktree still points to the branch declared in `slice.json.git.branch_name`
assumptions: The consumer repo has git and node available locally
The default feature and bugfix base branch remains `develop`
